questions.q: Gopal Krishna Gokhale is best known as a leader of which wing of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Gokhale was a leading moderate within the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Gopal Krishna Gokhale is famously remembered as the political mentor of which leader?
questions.explanation: Gandhi regarded Gokhale as his political teacher and guru.

questions.q: Which society did Gopal Krishna Gokhale found in 1905?
questions.explanation: Gokhale founded the Servants of India Society in 1905.

questions.q: Gokhale advocated achieving political change mainly through which approach?
questions.explanation: Gokhale favoured constitutional reform and gradual self-government within the British system.

questions.q: Because of his guidance to younger leaders, Gokhale is often called by which nickname?
questions.explanation: Gokhale is known as the 'Political Guru' for mentoring leaders such as Gandhi.

questions.q: Gokhale sought self-government for India within which framework?
questions.explanation: Gokhale sought gradual self-government for India within the British imperial framework.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Gopal Krishna Gokhale born?
questions.explanation: Gokhale was born in 1866 in the Ratnagiri region of present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Gokhale was a teacher by profession, working chiefly as a professor of which subject?
questions.explanation: Gokhale taught at Fergusson College, including subjects such as history, economics, and mathematics.

questions.q: Gokhale's preferred political methods were broadly the opposite of those of which assertive contemporary?
questions.explanation: Gokhale led the moderates, in contrast to the assertive nationalism of Bal Gangadhar Tilak.

questions.q: Gokhale was a prominent member of the legislative councils, where he was known for what?
options: Eloquent speeches and detailed budget critiques
questions.explanation: Gokhale was celebrated for his eloquent speeches and meticulous critiques of the government's budgets.

questions.q: Gokhale served as President of the Indian National Congress at which 1905 session?
questions.explanation: Gokhale presided over the 1905 Congress session held at Benares (Varanasi).

questions.q: Gokhale was an early political mentor of Gandhi, whom he advised to first do what on returning to India?
options: Travel across India to understand the country
questions.explanation: Gokhale advised Gandhi to spend a year travelling across India to understand its conditions before entering politics.

questions.q: Gokhale was a leading member of which prestigious British-era legislative body where he advocated reforms?
questions.explanation: Gokhale served in the Imperial Legislative Council, using it to press for reform.

questions.q: Gokhale was associated with which Poona college as a teacher for much of his career?
questions.explanation: Gokhale taught at Fergusson College in Poona, founded by the Deccan Education Society.

questions.q: Gokhale was influenced and politically guided early in his career by which veteran reformer of Poona?
questions.explanation: Justice M. G. Ranade was an important mentor who shaped Gokhale's moderate, reformist outlook.

questions.q: In which year did Gopal Krishna Gokhale die?
questions.explanation: Gokhale died in 1915, the same year Gandhi returned permanently to India.

questions.q: Gokhale strongly advocated which social reform measure for ordinary Indians?
questions.explanation: Gokhale championed free and compulsory primary education, even introducing a bill for it in the council.

questions.q: Gokhale was a member of the Deccan Education Society, which had earlier been co-founded by Tilak and which other leader?
questions.explanation: The Deccan Education Society was associated with founders such as Vishnushastri Chiplunkar and Tilak.

questions.q: Gokhale travelled to South Africa in 1912 partly to support whose campaign there?
questions.explanation: Gokhale visited South Africa in 1912 to lend support to Gandhi's struggle for the rights of Indians there.

questions.q: Gokhale was widely respected even by British officials for which quality in his political conduct?
options: His command of facts and reasoned argument
questions.explanation: Gokhale earned respect across the spectrum for his mastery of facts and reasoned, restrained argument.

questions.q: Which famous description did Gandhi reportedly use for Gokhale, comparing him to a sacred river?
options: He was 'pure as crystal' / like the Ganges
He was 'a tiger in the council'
questions.explanation: Gandhi praised Gokhale's purity of character, likening it to the clear, sacred waters of the Ganges.

questions.q: Gokhale's involvement with the Indian National Congress began in which decade?
questions.explanation: Gokhale became active in the Congress from the late 1880s and 1890s, rising as a leading moderate.

questions.q: Gokhale's celebrated annual speeches in the Imperial Legislative Council were most noted for their detailed analysis of what?
questions.explanation: Gokhale was renowned for his incisive annual critiques of the colonial government's budget.

questions.q: In 1912 Gokhale introduced a notable bill in the Imperial Legislative Council seeking to make which provision for children?
questions.explanation: Gokhale introduced an Elementary Education Bill in 1912 to provide for compulsory primary education, though it was defeated.

questions.q: Gokhale played a key role in shaping which set of constitutional reforms enacted in 1909?
questions.explanation: Gokhale's advocacy influenced the Morley-Minto (Indian Councils Act) Reforms of 1909.

questions.q: Gokhale's testimony before the Welby Commission in London concerned which subject?
questions.explanation: Gokhale testified before the Welby Commission on Indian expenditure, gaining a reputation for command of detail.

questions.q: Gokhale briefly edited or guided which English-language periodical associated with reformist opinion?
questions.explanation: Gokhale was associated with the reformist journal 'Sudharak,' founded by Gopal Ganesh Agarkar.

questions.q: A controversy in which Gokhale publicly apologized over a letter about police conduct during the 1897 plague is sometimes called what?
questions.explanation: Gokhale faced controversy and publicly apologized over allegations he had made regarding troops during the 1897 Poona plague operations.

questions.q: Gokhale rose to national prominence in the Bombay legislature and Congress partly by succeeding which mentor in public life?
questions.explanation: Gokhale closely followed and continued the work of his mentor M. G. Ranade in public and reform life.

questions.q: Gokhale's relatively early death at age 49 in 1915 meant he did not live to see which later phase of the freedom struggle?
options: The founding of the Congress in 1885
questions.explanation: Gokhale died in 1915, before Gandhi led the great mass movements of the 1920s through the 1940s.
